description: ClawHQ backlog — aligned with evolved strategy (2026-03-28). See docs/STRATEGY.md for strategic context.

items.title: Sentinel monitoring service — upstream intelligence that a cron job can't replicate
items.intent: ClawHQ must have a day-one revenue hypothesis. Sentinel is a paid monitoring service that provides upstream intelligence — pre-computing config breakage against incoming OpenClaw commits, tracking CVE impact per blueprint, and alerting on skill dependency changes. This must do something a local cron job cannot.
items.description: The evolved strategy requires testing revenue from day one, not deferring it. Sentinel is the hypothesis: ~$19/month monitoring that enhances sovereignty rather than compromising it. CRITICAL DESIGN CONSTRAINT: this must NOT be 'clawhq doctor --watch' as a service — that's replicable with a cron job and the sovereignty audience will call it out immediately. The value is upstream intelligence: (1) monitor OpenClaw GitHub commits and pre-compute whether your specific config will break before you update, (2) track new CVEs and map them to your installed skills/tools, (3) detect when upstream dependencies of your skills change, (4) aggregate anonymized health signals across the fleet to identify emerging issues before they hit you. Phase 1 (MVP): OpenClaw commit monitoring + config breakage prediction. Phase 2: CVE mapping + skill dependency tracking.
steps: Design Sentinel architecture: upstream monitor (GitHub API polling), config analyzer (diff against user's blueprint), alert delivery (email/webhook)
Implement OpenClaw commit monitor: poll GitHub API for new commits to main, parse changed files, identify config-impacting changes
Implement config breakage predictor: given a commit diff and a user's compiled config, determine if the update would break their deployment
Implement alert system: email or webhook notification with specific breakage details and recommended action
Design pricing page / signup flow for revenue validation
Build minimal web endpoint for Sentinel subscription management
Add clawhq cloud sentinel connect command to link a deployment to Sentinel
Phase 2: CVE tracker that maps advisories to user's specific skill/tool inventory
acceptanceCriteria: Sentinel monitors OpenClaw upstream commits and identifies config-impacting changes
Users receive alerts BEFORE updating when a new release would break their specific config
Alert includes: what changed upstream, what would break in their config, recommended action
Service provides value that a local cron job cannot replicate (upstream intelligence, not local health checks)
Pricing page exists and accepts signups for revenue validation
Revenue signal measurable within 30 days of announcement
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: Phase 1 only. GitHub API polling (commits to openclaw main), config diff engine (compare commit changes against user blueprint compiled config), webhook alert delivery. Minimal Express endpoint for subscription management. No pricing page in Phase 1 — free tier validates demand. clawhq cloud sentinel connect stores webhook URL.

items.title: Construct meta-skill — autonomous self-improvement loop for agent capability growth
items.intent: Agents must grow their capabilities over time without manual intervention. The construct skill is the mechanism: assess gaps → propose new skills → build → deploy → validate. This is THE differentiator for the 'agent grows' pillar.
items.description: Clawdius has a 'construct' skill that runs daily at 2am PT. Over 3 months it has autonomously built 7 skills. ClawHQ's skill system supports install/remove/update but has no autonomous growth mechanism.
steps: Design construct skill config for ClawHQ: SKILL.md, state machine (assess/propose/build/deploy/validate), cron schedule
Add construct to configs/skills/ as a built-in skill
Implement state persistence (construct tracks what it has assessed, proposed, built)
Wire construct into blueprint cron_config as an optional daily job
Add skill vetting integration — construct-built skills must pass the same vetting as manually installed skills
Add construct to 'replace-my-pa', 'founders-ops', and 'research-copilot' blueprints by default
Add tests for state machine transitions and skill output validation
acceptanceCriteria: Construct skill is available as a built-in skill in configs/skills/
Construct follows Assess→Propose→Build→Deploy→Validate cycle
Construct-built skills pass the same vetting pipeline as manually installed skills
State is persisted across runs (no redundant assessments)
Blueprints can opt-in to construct via skill_bundle configuration
At least 3 blueprints include construct by default
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: construct-built skills call vetSkill() from src/evolve/skills/. State in workspace/.construct-state.json. Assessed items tracked by ID+timestamp to avoid repeats.

items.title: Operational automation scripts — auto-update, security monitoring, workspace backup generation
items.intent: A deployed agent must maintain itself. Auto-updates, security advisory monitoring, and workspace backups should be generated by ClawHQ, not hand-written by the operator.
items.description: Clawdius has 24 operational scripts. ClawHQ has doctor and backup commands but does not generate the operational automation layer.
steps: Generate auto-update script during clawhq init
Generate security monitor script (checks GitHub Security Advisories for OpenClaw CVEs)
Generate workspace backup script (incremental rsync to configurable backup dir)
Generate systemd unit files for firewall watcher and backup timer
Add clawhq ops install command to deploy generated scripts and timers
Add doctor checks: auto-update timer active, backup recent, security monitor running
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq init generates operational scripts in the deployment directory
clawhq ops install deploys scripts as systemd timers/services
Auto-update runs on configurable schedule (default: daily)
Security monitor checks GitHub Advisories and alerts on critical/high CVEs
Workspace backup runs on configurable schedule with retention policy
clawhq doctor reports operational automation status
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: systemd units use clawhq-<instance>-<service>.timer naming from deploy dir basename. Multi-instance safe. No shared state.

items.title: Domain-based egress firewall with ipset — upgrade from IP-based iptables to DNS-resolved allowlists
items.intent: Agent egress must be restricted to explicitly allowed domains, not IP addresses. IP-based rules miss CDN rotations, multi-homed services, and IPv6.
items.description: ClawHQ's firewall module uses IP-based iptables rules. Clawdius uses domain-based egress: a domain allowlist resolved via DNS into an ipset, refreshed every 5 minutes.
steps: Add domain-allowlist concept to blueprint security_posture
Generate domain allowlist file from blueprint egress config during clawhq init
Implement ipset creation from domain allowlist
Update firewall module to use ipset match rules instead of per-IP iptables rules
Generate refresh mechanism for periodic DNS re-resolution
Add doctor check: ipset exists, not stale, matches allowlist
Add tests for domain resolution, ipset creation, stale detection
acceptanceCriteria: Blueprint security posture includes a domain allowlist
clawhq up creates an ipset from DNS resolution of the allowlist
iptables rules reference the ipset, not individual IP addresses
A refresh mechanism keeps the ipset current
clawhq doctor detects stale ipsets
clawhq down removes the ipset and iptables rules cleanly
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: ipset named clawhq_<instance>_egress per deploy dir basename. clawhq down destroys ipset. No shared state between instances. Multi-instance safe by construction.

items.title: Integration tests for credential probes — test against real API endpoints with test keys
items.intent: Credential health probes are tested with mocked fetch. If an API changes its auth error response, our probe will misinterpret it. Real probes against real APIs must be tested.
items.description: Live probe tests that run when test API keys are available. Ollama probe can always run locally.
steps: Add integration test: src/secure/credentials/probes.integration.test.ts
For each probe: if env var with test key is set, run real probe
Ollama probe can always run if Ollama is installed locally
Validate: probe returns valid:true for good keys, valid:false for bad keys
Skip gracefully when no test keys are available
acceptanceCriteria: Live probe tests run against real APIs when test credentials are available
Ollama probe runs against local Ollama when available
Tests skip gracefully when no credentials are provided
Existing mock-based unit tests remain unchanged
items.groomingNotes: Lower priority — probe API calls are simple HTTP and unlikely to break. Quick win for Ollama probe.

items.title: Stoic coach personality blueprint — purpose-built agent for philosophical mentorship
items.intent: The stoic coach blueprint proves ClawHQ can forge agents with deep personality — not just task automation, but genuine character. This is the blueprint that makes people say 'this is different from ChatGPT'.
items.description: Extract Clawdius's stoic identity patterns into a 'Stoic Coach' blueprint. Part of the '10 curated blueprints' strategy — this is blueprint #8.
steps: Create configs/blueprints/stoic-coach.yaml
Define personality: high analyticalDepth, moderate warmth, high directness, low verbosity
Define customization questions: philosophical tradition, practice times, accountability style
Define toolbelt: journal (append-only markdown), reflection-prompt generator
Define skills: daily-stoic (morning prompt + evening reflection), weekly-review
Define cron, autonomy, and memory policy
Test: clawhq init --guided with stoic-coach produces a deployable agent config
acceptanceCriteria: stoic-coach.yaml is a complete blueprint with all required sections
Personality dimensions are distinct from other blueprints
Blueprint includes at least one unique tool and one unique skill
clawhq init --guided with stoic-coach produces a deployable agent config
The forged agent has a distinctive voice — tested by reading generated SOUL.md
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: journal tool is append-only markdown writer (workspace/journal/YYYY-MM-DD.md). daily-stoic skill uses morning prompt + evening reflection cron pattern from existing morning-brief. Blueprint #11 (10 exist after FEAT-126). Sprint-ready.

items.title: JMAP email backend — FastMail-compatible email tool as alternative to himalaya/IMAP
items.intent: Users on FastMail (or any JMAP provider) must have a native email tool that uses JMAP instead of IMAP.
items.description: Clawdius has a battle-tested email-fastmail.py (stdlib Python, no deps) implementing JMAP. ClawHQ's email tool generator only produces himalaya-based IMAP scripts.
steps: Add JMAP email backend to src/design/tools/email.ts
Generate email-jmap.py (stdlib Python, no deps)
Add JMAP credential collection to wizard
Add account switching support (--account flag)
Add doctor check: JMAP session endpoint reachable, token valid
Add to email-manager and replace-my-pa blueprints
acceptanceCriteria: Email tool generator can produce JMAP-based scripts as alternative to himalaya
Generated JMAP tool uses stdlib Python only
Account switching works between JMAP and himalaya backends
clawhq doctor checks JMAP connectivity when configured
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: JMAP backend is a separate generated script (email-jmap.py). email.ts tool generator takes backend param (himalaya|jmap). Account switching via --account flag routes to correct backend. No coexistence issue — one backend per account.

items.title: News aggregator tool — multi-source scan with deduplication and interest-graph filtering
items.intent: Agents that monitor information sources need a unified news aggregator. Foundation for any monitoring or research blueprint.
items.description: Clawdius has a 'news' tool that scans X + Substack + Tavily by interest topic, deduplicates, and manages API budget.
steps: Design news tool API: scan by interest, deduplicate, budget management
Design interests tool API: add/remove topics, set priorities, manage sources
Add tool generators to src/design/tools/
Generate interests.json with default interests from blueprint
Add to research-copilot, founders-ops blueprints
Add tests for deduplication logic and budget management
acceptanceCriteria: News tool scans and returns deduplicated, filtered results
Interests tool manages a persistent interest graph
Generated tools use existing tool dependencies as backends
Budget management prevents runaway API costs
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: news tool wraps tavily + x tools. Dedup via URL hash set in news-state.json. Budget via max_calls_per_scan config. No new external deps needed — uses existing tool generators as backends.

items.title: Approval gates in identity generation — three-tier execute/propose/approve delegation model
items.intent: An agent with autonomy settings but no concrete action-level delegation rules will either over-ask or over-act. Generated AGENTS.md must include explicit approval gates with per-action examples.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): AGENTS.md has three-tier approval model with concrete per-action examples. ClawHQ generateAgents() only outputs tools/skills inventory.
steps: Extend blueprint autonomy_model with per-action delegation defaults
Update generateAgents() in src/design/identity/agents.ts to emit approval gates section
Add heartbeat behavior patterns section
Add communication rules section
Add tests: each autonomy level produces correct delegation tiers
acceptanceCriteria: Generated AGENTS.md includes three-tier approval gates with per-action examples
Autonomy level (low/medium/high) maps to different delegation defaults
Heartbeat behavior patterns documented in generated AGENTS.md
items.groomingNotes: Sprint-ready after codebase analysis. generateAgents() in src/design/identity/agents.ts is 78 lines — confirmed gap: emits only name/role/tools/skills/autonomy-list. Missing: three-tier delegation (execute/propose/approve), per-action examples, heartbeat behavior section, communication rules. Blueprint autonomy_model must be extended with delegation_defaults[]. Implementation: (1) extend Blueprint type with delegation_defaults field, (2) add generateDelegationGates() function, (3) emit 3-section Approval Gates block in generateAgents(), (4) add test file. Scope is contained: agents.ts + blueprint types + tests. Autonomy level mapping: low=approve-most, medium=balanced, high=execute-most.

items.title: Credential proxy architecture — host-side token proxy so secrets never enter the container
items.intent: Storing API tokens inside the agent container is a single-exploit-away from credential theft. A host-side credential proxy means the container never holds tokens.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): cred-proxy on Docker bridge holds all API tokens on host, injects auth headers. ClawHQ has src/secure/credentials/ but no proxy architecture.
steps: Design credential proxy service: Docker sidecar, route definitions, header injection
Add routes.json schema
Generate cred-proxy docker-compose service during clawhq init
Update tool generators to call cred-proxy URL
Add audit logging for proxied requests
Add doctor check: cred-proxy reachable, routes healthy
acceptanceCriteria: Credential proxy runs as host-side sidecar
API tokens never in agent container
Generated tools call proxy URL
Proxied requests audit-logged
clawhq doctor checks proxy health
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: Docker sidecar on bridge (172.x.0.1:8900). routes.json: {tool: {backend_url, auth_header, token_env_var}}. Tools use CRED_PROXY_URL. /health for liveness. Doctor checks proxy reachable.

items.title: Delegated action rules — blueprint-level pre-approved action categories with pattern matching
items.intent: Users must define what their agent can do without asking. Email delegation is the first use case but the pattern generalizes.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): delegated-send-rules.json defines pre-approved categories. ClawHQ has no action-level delegation config.
steps: Design delegated-action-rules schema
Add delegation_rules to blueprint spec
Implement rule generation during init
Generate delegated-rules.json in workspace
Add email delegation defaults to email-manager blueprint
Add tests
acceptanceCriteria: Blueprints can define delegated action rules per tool
Generated workspace includes delegated-rules.json
Email-manager ships with email delegation defaults
Rules support pattern matching
items.groomingNotes: Confirmed: Blueprint interface has no delegation_rules field. generate.ts does not emit delegated-rules.json. Implementation path: (1) Design delegated-action-rules schema — per-tool rule entries with: action name, pattern (glob or regex), auto_approve bool, escalation_contact optional. Add DelegationRules interface to src/design/blueprints/types.ts. (2) Add optional delegation_rules field to Blueprint interface. (3) Add rule generation to generate.ts: compile blueprint.delegation_rules into workspace/delegated-rules.json during init. (4) Add email-manager defaults: auto-approve read/triage/label, escalate send/delete/forward-external. (5) Tests: email-manager blueprint generates delegated-rules.json with expected entries; pattern matching test (glob-based action names). Sprint-ready: all scope files exist, no external dependencies, no ipset or Docker required.

items.title: Interest graph config — topic-driven content filtering with multi-source routing

steps: Design interests.json schema
Add interests config to blueprint spec
Add interests tool generator
Generate interests.json from blueprint
Wire into morning-brief and market-scan skills
acceptanceCriteria: Blueprints can define default interest topics
Generated workspace includes interests.json
Interests tool allows runtime management
items.groomingNotes: Design resolved: interests.json schema: {topics: [{id, label, priority, frequency, sources: {x_handles?, tavily_queries?, substack_pubs?}}]}. Blueprint spec gets optional interests section. interests CLI tool: add/remove/list/prioritize.

items.title: Per-blueprint domain egress allowlists — blueprint-aware firewall defaults
items.intent: Each blueprint knows what services it needs. Firewall allowlist should be generated from the blueprint.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): domain allowlist per integration. FEAT-116 covers ipset but not per-blueprint defaults.
steps: Map integration types to required domains
Add domain_allowlist generation to generate.ts
Compile integrations+tools to allowlist
Generate allowlist file during init
Add doctor check for missing domains
acceptanceCriteria: Each blueprint compiles to a domain allowlist
clawhq init generates allowlist automatically
Doctor detects missing domains
items.groomingNotes: NOT BLOCKED BY FEAT-116. This item uses existing domain-based iptables rules already in firewall.ts — no ipset needed. FEAT-116 dependency is for the future periodic DNS-refresh upgrade only; this item is unblocked. dependsOn field will be cleared when CLI supports it. Implementation: in generate.ts, iterate blueprint.security_posture.egress_domains and emit allowlist.yaml (default port 443, MTA domains get 587/465). Add doctor check: each blueprint domain must exist in allowlist.yaml.

items.title: ClawWall coverage parity — audit sanitizer against Clawdius production firewall
items.intent: ClawHQ sanitizer must match or exceed battle-tested production firewall. Gaps mean real injection vectors.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): sanitize.py 1,329 lines, OWASP LLM01 Tier 1+2. ClawHQ has src/secure/sanitizer/ — audit delta.
steps: Catalog Clawdius sanitize.py detection patterns
Catalog ClawHQ sanitizer coverage
Identify gaps
Implement missing patterns
Add test cases
acceptanceCriteria: All detection categories covered
OWASP LLM01 Tier 1+2 tested
Obfuscation detection covered
No regression
items.groomingNotes: Gap analysis complete: 11 gaps found. P0: egress secret scanning (last line of data sovereignty defense), indirect elicitation (credential leak via social engineering). P1: semantic override, soft instruction override, leetspeak normalization, extended unicode. P2: split-payload detection, context poisoning, self-integrity. P3: Q/A few-shot, LLM classifier (future). Sprint plan: P0 gaps first, then P1.

items.title: Cron model routing validation — verify FEAT-113 against Clawdius production model map
items.intent: FEAT-113 defaults must be validated against real production patterns.

steps: Document Clawdius model-to-job mapping
Compare against ClawHQ defaults
Update where Clawdius patterns are better
Add validation for cost-inefficient assignments
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 blueprints model assignments reviewed
High-frequency jobs default to haiku
Complex jobs default to opus
Validation warns on cost-inefficient assignments
items.groomingNotes: Codebase analysis: src/config/validate.ts exists, validate.test.ts exists. FEAT-113 added model routing per cron job. This item validates those defaults against Clawdius production patterns (haiku for fast, sonnet for medium, opus for complex). Implementation: (1) Document Clawdius model map in groomingNotes for reference: morning-brief=haiku, triage=haiku, market-scan=sonnet, construct=opus. (2) Update blueprint cron_config model assignments where wrong. (3) Add validate.ts rule: warn if cron schedule is sub-hourly (<=60min) and model is opus or sonnet.

items.title: Silence-is-competence identity pattern — operational philosophy in SOUL.md generation
items.intent: An agent that narrates every action wastes attention. SOUL.md must encode execution philosophy.
items.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): SOUL.md encodes silence-is-competence, quality gates, guardian model. ClawHQ generateSoul() lacks this layer.
steps: Add execution_philosophy section to SOUL.md generator
Define philosophy rules
Make configurable via proactivity dimension
Add guardian model for high-autonomy blueprints
Add tests
acceptanceCriteria: Generated SOUL.md includes execution philosophy
Philosophy scales with proactivity
Guardian model included for high-autonomy
Token budget still passes
items.groomingNotes: Sprint-ready after codebase analysis. generateSoul() in src/design/identity/soul.ts is 160 lines — confirmed gap: generates dimension prose (analyticalDepth, warmth, directness, verbosity, proactivity, creativity, structure) but no execution_philosophy section. The proactivity dimension (lines 26-41) is already computed — execution philosophy should map proactivity<=2 to 'ask before act', proactivity>=4 to 'silence is competence'. Guardian model section should be added for autonomy_model.default=high blueprints. Implementation: add generateExecutionPhilosophy(blueprint) helper, add guardian model conditional, wire into main generateSoul() output, add tests. Scope: soul.ts only. Token budget must still pass — add token budget test assertion.
